schools.summary: Gill: there was no supreme magistrate or judge, this repeated to account for the disorders; all their inheritance had not fallen to them.

themes: In those days there was no king in Israel... the tribe of the Danites sought them an inheritance to dwell in
the want of godly rule breeding disorder, the unpossessed inheritance breeding wandering
where there is no vision, the people perish (Prov. 29:18)

schools.summary: Gill: they sent five men, as Moses sent spies to search the land; they came to the house of Micah and lodged there.

themes: the children of Dan sent of their family five men... to spy out the land... and they came to... the house of Micah, and lodged there
men's ways overruled to God's purpose, yet the snare of lodging near idolatry
a companion of fools shall be destroyed (Prov. 13:20)

schools.summary: Gill: they were at their inn next to Micah's house, or passing by it.
Keil & Delitzsch: they knew the voice of the young Levite and questioned him.

themes: they knew the voice of the young man the Levite... Who brought thee hither? and what makest thou in this place? and what hast thou here?
the servant of God questioned when found in unworthy employ
how is the faithful city become an harlot! (Isa. 1:21)

schools.summary: Gill: he told them the whole story; he hath hired me, and I am his priest.

themes: Thus and thus dealeth Micah with me, and hath hired me, and I am his priest
the shame of a hireling ministry serving for gain rather than for God
the hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep (John 10:13)

schools.summary: Gill: they did not reprove him for assuming the priestly office, such was the corruption of those times; asked whether their way would prosper.

themes: Ask counsel, we pray thee, of God, that we may know whether our way which we go shall be prosperous
a form of seeking God while using means he abhors
should not a people seek unto their God? for the living to the dead? (Isa. 8:19)

schools.summary: Gill: he consulted the ephod and teraphim, or spoke of his own head, or by a voice Satan might be permitted to deliver.

themes: Go in peace: before the LORD is your way wherein ye go
the peril of false prophets who speak peace where God has not spoken
they prophesy unto you a false vision... and the deceit of their heart (Jer. 14:14)

schools.summary: Gill: they came to Laish after viewing it well.
Henry: the observation the spies made on the city and the posture of its inhabitants.

themes: came to Laish, and saw the people... dwelt careless... quiet and secure... and there was no magistrate in the land
the false security of the careless exposing them to sudden ruin
when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh (1 Thess. 5:3)

schools.summary: Gill: they returned after viewing the city and its circumstances; their brethren said, What say ye?

themes: they came unto their brethren to Zorah and Eshtaol: and their brethren said unto them, What say ye?
the well-ordered venture tainted at the root by a false oracle and idolatry
the sacrifice of the wicked is abomination (Prov. 21:27)

schools.summary: Gill: they urged them to prepare for war and go up against the present possessors.
Keil & Delitzsch: be not slothful to go.

themes: Arise, that we may go up against them: for we have seen the land... it is very good... be not slothful to go
the world's diligence in earthly gain a rebuke to the slackness of God's people
be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith... inherit the promises (Heb. 6:12)

schools.summary: Gill: the territories were large and the people secure, easily mastered; a place where there is no want of any thing.

themes: God hath given it into your hands; a place where there is no want of any thing that is in the earth
presuming upon God's blessing for a venture born of idolatry
that... have not asked at my mouth (Isa. 30:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the singular is put for the plural, for such a number could hardly come from one family; six hundred men with weapons of war.

themes: there went from thence of the family of the Danites... six hundred men appointed with weapons of war
the lawless violence of an age without a king, where might made right
imputing this his power unto his god (Hab. 1:11)

schools.summary: Gill: it was sixteen miles from Zorah, their first day's march.
Keil & Delitzsch: them naming it Mahaneh-dan.

themes: they went up, and pitched in Kirjathjearim, in Judah... they called that place Mahanehdan unto this day
not every venture men commemorate is worthy of the LORD
they call their lands after their own names (Ps. 49:11)

schools.summary: Gill: they steered their course northward, eight miles from Kirjath-jearim.
Keil & Delitzsch: them coming to the house of Micah.

themes: And they passed thence unto mount Ephraim, and came unto the house of Micah
the path of the covetous leading to the occasions of further sin
evil pursueth sinners: but to the righteous good shall be repayed (Prov. 13:21)

schools.summary: Gill: the five men had some notion of Laish as proper for them; they said, consider what ye have to do.

themes: there is in these houses an ephod, and teraphim, and a graven image... now therefore consider what ye have to do
the covetous heart adding sacrilege to robbery for false worship
supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself (1 Tim. 6:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the house lay a little out of their way, so they turned to it.
Keil & Delitzsch: them coming to the house and saluting him.

themes: they turned thitherward, and came to the house of the young man the Levite... and saluted him
fair words masking a foul intent to rob and ensnare
the words of his mouth were softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords (Ps. 55:21)

schools.summary: Gill: they were armed men, marching with their armour about them.
Keil & Delitzsch: them standing by the entering of the gate.

themes: the six hundred men appointed with their weapons of war... stood by the entering of the gate
the oppression of the strong against the weak in lawless times
the violent man... they have purposed to overthrow my goings (Ps. 140:4)

schools.summary: Gill: they first came to Micah's house and saluted the young man; took the graven image, ephod, teraphim, and molten image.

themes: the five men... took the graven image, and the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image
the impotence of idols that cannot defend themselves or their house
they must needs be borne, because they cannot go... neither... can they do good (Jer. 10:5)

schools.summary: Gill: it was the five men who knew the house and the chapel where the things were.
Keil & Delitzsch: the priest said, What do ye?

themes: these went into Micah's house, and fetched the carved image... Then said the priest unto them, What do ye?
a conscience stirred too late, only for self-interest, not for truth
they all look to their own way, every one for his gain (Isa. 56:11)

schools.summary: Gill: they bid him be silent and quiet.
Clarke: laying the hand on the mouth was the token of silence.

themes: go with us, and be to us a father and a priest: is it better... to be a priest unto the house of one man, or... unto a tribe?
ambition and covetousness drawing men from one false service to a larger
they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare (1 Tim. 6:9)

schools.summary: Gill: he rejoiced at the opportunity, suiting his covetous, ambitious, unsettled disposition; went in the midst of the people.

themes: And the priest's heart was glad, and he took the ephod... and went in the midst of the people
the baseness of a heart ruled by gain, faithless and shameless
the love of money is the root of all evil (1 Tim. 6:10)

schools.summary: Gill: they turned from the gate and marched forward to Laish.
Keil & Delitzsch: them putting the little ones and cattle before them.

themes: So they turned and departed, and put the little ones and the cattle and the carriage before them
the guilty awareness of those who do wrong, fearing pursuit
the wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion (Prov. 28:1)

schools.summary: Gill: it took time before Micah knew his gods were stolen and his priest decoyed away; the men near Micah's house overtook the Danites.

themes: the men that were in the houses near to Micah's house were gathered together, and overtook the children of Dan
the idolater's heart set on his vain trust, pursuing after gods that cannot help
they that make a graven image are all of them vanity (Isa. 44:9)

schools.summary: Gill: they called aloud, desiring them to stop.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Danites asking, What aileth thee?

themes: they turned their faces, and said unto Micah, What aileth thee, that thou comest with such a company?
the brazen effrontery of those who sin without shame
she... saith, I have done no wickedness (Prov. 30:20)

schools.summary: Gill: he meant his graven and molten images, which he had made or paid for; what have I more?

themes: Ye have taken away my gods which I made, and the priest... and what have I more?
the absurdity of worshipping what one's own hands have made, left desolate when it fails
they are vanity, and the work of errors: in the time of their visitation they shall perish (Jer. 10:15)

schools.summary: Gill: they bid him not complain of them as having done an unjust thing; threatened his life and his household.

themes: Let not thy voice be heard among us, lest angry fellows run upon thee, and thou lose thy life
the lawless adding murder's threat to theft, crushing any who resist
wasting and destruction are in their paths (Isa. 59:7)

schools.summary: Gill: they bid him defiance, and Micah, being too weak, returned.
Keil & Delitzsch: Micah turning back to his house.

themes: when Micah saw that they were too strong for him, he turned and went back unto his house
the idolater left with nothing in the day of loss, his vain confidence stripped
the LORD alone shall be exalted... and the idols he shall utterly abolish (Isa. 2:17-18)

schools.summary: Gill: the Danites kept the things Micah made and went on with them.
Keil & Delitzsch: them smiting Laish and burning the city.

themes: came unto Laish, unto a people that were at quiet and secure: and they smote them with the edge of the sword, and burnt the city
the sudden destruction of those who dwell at ease without God
while they be folden together as thorns... they shall be devoured (Nah. 1:10)

schools.summary: Gill: it was far from Zidon, under whose protection they were, who could not help suddenly; they built a city and dwelt therein.

themes: And there was no deliverer, because it was far from Zidon... And they built a city, and dwelt therein
the misery of those who, far from God, have no saviour in their extremity
be not far from me; for trouble is near; for there is none to help (Ps. 22:11)

schools.summary: Gill: they named it to show that, though at the furthest part of the land, they were of the tribe of Dan; the name was Laish at the first.

themes: they called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father... howbeit the name of the city was Laish at the first
a place soon notorious for idolatry; a name honoured yet defiled by sin
they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass (Ps. 106:20)

schools.summary: Gill: very probably a house was built for it, in the same place where Jeroboam later set up a golden calf.
Keil & Delitzsch: Jonathan and his sons were priests to Dan until the captivity.

themes: the children of Dan set up the graven image: and Jonathan, the son of Gershom, the son of Manasseh, he and his sons were priests to the tribe of Dan
even the line of Moses falling into idol-priesthood; grace not inherited
the soul that sinneth, it shall die... the son shall not bear the iniquity of the father (Ezek. 18:20)

schools.summary: Gill: this is repeated for the sake of the time of its continuance.
Keil & Delitzsch: it stood all the time the house of God was in Shiloh.

themes: they set them up Micah's graven image, which he made, all the time that the house of God was in Shiloh
the sad coexistence of idolatry with the true worship of God
ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils (1 Cor. 10:21)
